---
title: "country_wiseID"
author: "Prerana"
---

```{r}
# compute_countries_by_article_case.R
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(writexl)  # install.packages("writexl") if needed
library(readr)
```

```{r}
# --- input / output paths ---
infile <- "output/processed_data_aw.xlsx"
out_dir <- "output"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# --- read data ---
df <- read_xlsx(infile, .name_repair = "unique")
```

```{r}
# --- find likely column names for ArticleID, CaseID, Country ---
col_article <- names(df)[which(str_detect(tolower(names(df)), "articleid|1_article|1article"))[1]]
col_case    <- names(df)[which(str_detect(tolower(names(df)), "caseid|1.1_caseid|1_1_caseid"))[1]]
col_country <- names(df)[which(str_detect(tolower(names(df)), "cnt|country|country_name|18_cnt"))[1]]

# safety: if any not found try fallback exact names
if (is.na(col_article) || length(col_article) == 0) {
  if ("1_ID" %in% names(df)) col_article <- "1_ID"
}
if (is.na(col_case) || length(col_case) == 0) {
  if ("1.1_CaseID" %in% names(df)) col_case <- "1.1_CaseID"
}
if (is.na(col_country) || length(col_country) == 0) {
  if ("16_CNT" %in% names(df)) col_country <- "16_CNT"
}

# stop if we still couldn't detect a country column
if (is.na(col_country) || length(col_country) == 0) {
  stop("Could not detect a country column in the dataset. Check column names in processed_data_aw.xlsx")
}

# --- normalize/clean country values ---
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    country_raw = as.character(.data[[col_country]]),
    country_clean = country_raw %>%
      str_replace_all("\\s*;\\s*", "; ") %>%   # normalize separators
      str_replace_all("\\bNA\\b|^NA$|NA4", "") %>% # remove NA tokens
      str_trim() %>%
      na_if("") %>%
      # unify capitalization
      str_to_title()
  )

# --- countries per article ---
countries_per_article <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(country_clean)) %>%
  group_by(article = .data[[col_article]]) %>%
  summarise(
    n_countries = n_distinct(country_clean),
    countries = paste(sort(unique(country_clean)), collapse = "; "),
    n_cases = n_distinct(.data[[col_case]]),   # optional: how many case-ids in article
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_countries), article)

# --- countries per (article + case) ---
countries_per_case <- df %>%
  # keep only rows where case id exists
  filter(!is.na(.data[[col_case]])) %>%
  group_by(article = .data[[col_article]], case = .data[[col_case]]) %>%
  summarise(
    n_countries = n_distinct(country_clean[!is.na(country_clean)]),
    countries = paste(sort(unique(country_clean[!is.na(country_clean)])), collapse = "; "),
    n_rows = n(),   # how many rows correspond to this (article,case)
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(article, case)
```

```{r}
# --- save outputs ---
write_csv(countries_per_article, file.path(out_dir, "countries_per_article.csv"))
write_csv(countries_per_case,    file.path(out_dir, "countries_per_case.csv"))

# also write both sheets into one Excel workbook
write_xlsx(
  list(
    countries_per_article = countries_per_article,
    countries_per_case = countries_per_case
  ),
  path = file.path(out_dir, "article_case_countries.xlsx")
)
```

```{r}
# Summary 

# total counts
n_articles <- nrow(countries_per_article)
n_cases    <- if (exists("countries_per_case") && nrow(countries_per_case) > 0) nrow(countries_per_case) else 0

# total distinct countries across all rows (robust to empty/NA)
all_country_list <- unlist(
  strsplit(
    na.omit(countries_per_article$countries[countries_per_article$countries != ""]),
    split = ";\\s*"
  )
)
total_distinct_countries <- length(unique(trimws(all_country_list)))

cat("=== Summary: countries per article / case ===\n")
cat("Articles processed (rows in countries_per_article):", n_articles, "\n")
cat("Cases processed (rows in countries_per_case):", n_cases, "\n")
cat("Total distinct countries found across all articles:", total_distinct_countries, "\n\n")

# distribution statistics for countries per article
if (n_articles > 0) {
  cat("Countries per article - summary:\n")
  print(summary(countries_per_article$n_countries))
  cat("\nNumber of articles with at least one country:", sum(countries_per_article$n_countries > 0), "\n\n")
}

# top 10 articles by number of countries
if (n_articles > 0) {
  cat("Top articles by distinct country count (top 10):\n")
  top_articles <- countries_per_article %>%
    arrange(desc(n_countries)) %>%
    slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    select(article, n_countries, countries)
  print(top_articles)
  cat("\n")
}

# distribution and top for cases (if available)
if (n_cases > 0) {
  cat("Countries per (article,case) - summary:\n")
  print(summary(countries_per_case$n_countries))
  cat("\nTop (article, case) rows by country count (top 10):\n")
  top_cases <- countries_per_case %>%
    arrange(desc(n_countries)) %>%
    slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    select(article, case, n_countries, countries)
  print(top_cases)
  cat("\n")
}


```
